title: buinalis rugelii
intro: Buinalis rugelii, commonly known as Rugel's oak moss or Baekea rugelii, is a species of lichenized fungus in the family Roccellaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties and is used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Buinalis rugelii include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of Buinalis rugelii include Roccellin A, Roccellin B, and other Roccellaceae-specific compounds that contribute to its therapeutic actions, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
side_effects: Buinalis rugelii can have side effects if used improperly, such as diarrhea, stomach pain, and nausea, particularly when ingested in large quantities. In rare cases, excessive consumption of the plant's extracts can lead to kidney damage and liver toxicity. Prolonged use or misuse of buinalis rugelii has also been linked to allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and respiratory issues in some individuals. If not handled correctly, the plant's sap may cause irritation to the skin and eyes, potentially leading to long-term damage.
